body_txt: In-laws, whether they're obnoxious or friendly , are an inevitable part of almost any marriage. Because of this, people who think of themselves as someone's future spouse will sometimes refer to their crush's relatives as their future in-laws. Characters with this mentality often do this either out of a desire to kiss up to their crush's family or out of a sense of arrogance. If the "in-law" dislikes their family's pursuer, expect the latter to insist that they should get along because they'll be family in the future. If a person is particularly certain that they'll marry their crush in the future they might actually treat their crush's family like they already are in-laws. A somewhat less common variant of this trope involves a person who ships their family member with someone referring to the latter as a future in-law. The latter person might be someone the family member has an unrequited crush on, a person who has a crush on someone's family member, or someone who a person wants to get with their family. See also Mrs. Hypothetical for self-proclaimed future spouses. See Self-Proclaimed Love Interest , Parental Relationship Approval , Abhorrent Admirer , Fair-Weather Ex , Stalker with a Crush , Declaration of Spousehood , and Entitled to Have You for characters who are likely to have this attitude. Not to be confused with Phony Relative .
